payload.title: MTA Board Action Items
payload.description: MTA Board Meeting materials including board action items, procurement packages, real estate items, capital program amendments, fare-free bus pilot program, and e-hail program Phase 2 Expansion

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: Hon. Janno Lieber, Chair & CEO MTA

payload.description: Part NN of Chapter 56 of the New York Laws of 2023 requires MTA to establish one fare-free bus route in each county of NYC, operating for a minimum of six months and a maximum of one year
Triple enrollment in the E-hail program from 1,200 to 3,600 customers; introduces limits on subsidized rides and introduces Distance and High-Volume options
99-year ground lease with Boston Properties for redevelopment of former MTA headquarters at 341-347 Madison Avenue into commercial office tower with new ESA entrance and transit improvements
payload.location: 341-347 Madison Avenue, between East 44th and 45th Streets, New York, NY

location_normalized.raw_text: 341-347 Madison Avenue, between East 44th and 45th Streets, New York, NY

tool_args.label: Hudson Tunnel Project - Segment 3 Concrete Casing

payload.project_name: Hudson Tunnel Project - Segment 3 Concrete Casing
payload.description: Construction of Segment 3 concrete casing for Amtrak's Hudson Tunnel Project under the Western Rail Yard portion of the John D. Caemmerer West Side Storage Yard

Changes funding mix with no change in total funding amount of $33.913 billion; increases Federal New Starts funding by $900 million and MTA Bonds by $643 million through transfers from 2020-2024 Capital Program
Moves $878 million from core agencies to Network Expansion: $678 million to Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 and $200 million to Penn Station Access; overall budget unchanged at $55.442 billion

tool_args.label: Sale of Development Rights in Port Morris, Bronx

payload.project_name: Sale of Development Rights in Port Morris, Bronx
payload.description: Sale of ±55,174 square feet of excess transferrable development rights at East 135th Street and Park Avenue to Waterfront Living II, LLC for ±$2,483,300
payload.location: East 135 Street and Park Avenue, Port Morris, Bronx

location_normalized.raw_text: East 135 Street and Park Avenue, Port Morris, Bronx

tool_args.label: Fare-Free Bus Pilot Program Start Date

payload.description: The fare-free bus pilot program and the fare-free paratransit pilot program will begin on or before September 25, 2023
E-hail Phase 2 Expansion will begin on or around August 1, 2023

tool_args.label: Fare-Free Bus Pilot Net Operating Cost Limit

payload.description: Net operating costs of the fare-free bus pilot program is legally mandated not to exceed $15,000,000

tool_args.label: Fare-Free Paratransit Pilot Net Operating Cost Limit

payload.description: Net operating costs of the fare-free paratransit pilot program is not expected to exceed $700,000
Current annual budget for E-hail Phase 1 is $16,000,000 for 1,200 enrollees

payload.raw_value_text: an additional $1.6 million more for 3,600 enrollees
payload.description: Phase 2 Expansion is projected to cost an additional $1.6 million more for 3,600 enrollees

payload.raw_value_text: 13 actions for a proposed expenditure of $30.5 M
payload.description: C&D Procurement Agenda includes 13 actions for a proposed expenditure of $30.5 million

payload.raw_value_text: 1 action for a proposed estimated expenditure of $17.6M
payload.description: MTA Headquarters Procurement Agenda includes 1 action for a proposed estimated expenditure of $17.6 million
MTA Board Meeting at 2 Broadway 20th Floor Board Room New York, NY 10004
Total amount for four biennial bridge inspection personal services contracts for MTA Bridges & Tunnels
Option exercise for design, manufacturing, testing, and delivery of six dual-mode locomotives for CDOT, fully funded by CDOT
Modification to extend Cubic transportation contract for BusCIS Hardware Subsystem in-depot maintenance services for four years

payload.raw_value_text: 1 action for a proposed estimated expenditure of $78.8M
payload.description: Metro-North Railroad Procurement Agenda includes 1 action for a proposed estimated expenditure of $78.8 million
Total payment for 2023 State Public Work Enforcement Fund assessment including adjustment for 2022 underpayment

tool_args.label: ABLE Legislation for Bus Lane Enforcement

payload.claim_text: The budget included the ABLE legislation -- part of a serious strategy to improve bus speeds and performance. This legislation will not only subject people who block bus lanes to automatic ticketing that comes from a camera but will also apply to drivers who are doubled parked in a bus stop for an extended period of time.
payload.subject: ABLE legislation
payload.description: Chair Lieber announced that the budget included ABLE legislation for automated bus lane enforcement and double parking enforcement at bus stops

payload.claim_text: MTA is losing close to $700 million a year right now to fare and toll evasion
payload.subject: fare and toll evasion
payload.description: Chair Lieber stated MTA loses close to $700 million a year to fare and toll evasion

payload.claim_text: The other big news is obviously congestion pricing – the release of the Final Environmental Assessment ('EA'). MTA is moving closer to final approval of its version of the Central Business District Tolling ('CBDT') program
payload.subject: congestion pricing / CBDT
payload.description: Chair Lieber announced release of Final Environmental Assessment for Central Business District Tolling

payload.claim_text: In the Distance option, the MTA subsidy will apply to up to 25 trips per month. After the first $4 of trip cost, MTA will be responsible for the next $40 of trip cost, and the customer will be responsible for any trip cost beyond $44.
payload.subject: E-hail Distance option
payload.description: Distance option: up to 25 subsidized trips per month, customer pays first $4, MTA pays next $40

payload.claim_text: In the High-Volume option, the MTA subsidy will apply to up to 40 trips per month. After the first $4 of trip cost, the MTA will be responsible for the next $25 of trip cost, and the customer will be responsible for any trip cost beyond $29.
payload.subject: E-hail High-Volume option
payload.description: High-Volume option: up to 40 subsidized trips per month, customer pays first $4, MTA pays next $25

payload.raw_value_text: 6,000 miles in the 70s to 120,000 miles today; currently purchasing cars operating at 220,000 miles MDBF
payload.description: Improvement in subway car mean distance between failures from 6,000 miles in the 1970s to 120,000 miles currently; new cars operate at 220,000 miles
Metro-North set a record with 214,000 riders
